acu-points.title: Spleen Channel (SP) — Foot Tai Yin
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the medial side of the big toe tip (SP1), runs along the medial border of the foot, ascends in front of the medial malleolus, up the medial leg, crosses to the anterior aspect at SP6 (3 cun above the malleolus, in front of the Liver channel), continues up the medial thigh, enters the abdomen, connects with the Spleen and Stomach, ascends through the diaphragm, spreads along the sides of the esophagus, and connects to the root of the tongue. A branch separates from the stomach and ascends through the diaphragm to pour into the Heart, connecting with the Heart channel. The main external branch continues from the abdomen up the chest wall, ending at SP21 on the mid-axillary line.

items.t: Foot Tai Yin, paired (exterior-interior) with the Stomach channel
Fourth channel in the 12-channel Qi circulation, following the Stomach channel
Governs transformation and transportation of food/fluids, and holding blood within the vessels; heavily used for gynecological, digestive, and damp-related disorders

acu-points.title: Heart Channel (HT) — Hand Shao Yin
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts in the Heart, spreads through the "heart system", descends through the diaphragm to connect with the Small Intestine. A branch ascends from the heart system beside the throat to the eye. The main external branch emerges from the heart system, ascends to the lung, and emerges in the axilla (HT1), runs down the posterior-medial aspect of the arm (behind LU and PC), along the medial epicondyle of the humerus, along the ulnar side of the forearm, across the wrist, along the palm, ending at the radial side of the little finger tip (HT9), connecting to the Small Intestine channel.

items.t: Hand Shao Yin, paired (exterior-interior) with the Small Intestine channel
Only 9 points, all below the elbow (HT1 is the exception, at the axilla) — a short but clinically important channel
Governs blood and vessels, and houses the Shen (spirit/mind) — heavily used for insomnia, palpitations, and anxiety

acu-points.title: Pericardium Channel (PC) — Hand Jue Yin
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts in the chest, emerges and connects with the Pericardium, descends through the diaphragm, connecting successively with the Upper, Middle, and Lower Jiao (Sanjiao). A branch runs across the chest, emerges below the axilla (PC1), ascends to the axilla, then descends along the medial aspect of the arm, between the Lung and Heart channels, through the middle of the cubital fossa, down the forearm between the tendons of palmaris longus and flexor carpi radialis, into the palm, ending at the tip of the middle finger (PC9). A branch leaves PC8 (palm) to the tip of the ring finger, connecting to the Sanjiao channel.

items.t: Hand Jue Yin, paired (exterior-interior) with the Sanjiao (Triple Energizer) channel
9 points — closely parallels the Heart channel but runs along the middle (not posterior) aspect of the arm
The Pericardium is described as the "protector" of the Heart — its channel is used for many of the same disorders (chest/cardiac symptoms) plus stomach-related nausea/vomiting

acu-points.title: Small Intestine Channel (SI) — Hand Tai Yang
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the ulnar side of the little finger tip (SI1), runs along the ulnar side of the hand and forearm, between the olecranon and the medial epicondyle of the humerus, up the posterior-lateral aspect of the upper arm to the shoulder, zigzags across the scapula, meets Du Mai at GV14, then goes to the supraclavicular fossa, connects with the Heart, descends through the diaphragm to the Stomach, and enters the Small Intestine. A branch runs from the supraclavicular fossa up the neck to the cheek, outer canthus, and into the ear (SI19); another branch runs from the cheek to the inner canthus, connecting to the Bladder channel.

items.t: Hand Tai Yang, paired (exterior-interior) with the Heart channel
19 points; SI3 (Houxi) is the Confluent point of Du Mai and a classic point for the spine and neck
Governs "separating the clear from the turbid" in digestion; clinically used heavily for the shoulder/scapula and ear

acu-points.title: Bladder Channel (BL) — Foot Tai Yang
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the inner canthus (BL1), ascends over the forehead to the vertex (meeting Du Mai at GV20). A branch goes to the top of the ear; the main pathway enters the brain at the occiput, descends along the back in two parallel lines — 1.5 cun and 3 cun lateral to the spine — passing over all the Back-Shu points, through the buttock, down the posterior thigh, crosses the popliteal fossa (BL40), continues down the calf, behind the lateral malleolus, along the lateral border of the foot, ending at the lateral side of the little toe (BL67), connecting to the Kidney channel.

items.t: Foot Tai Yang, paired (exterior-interior) with the Kidney channel
The longest channel in the body — 67 points, running the entire length from the eye to the little toe
Carries all 12 Back-Shu points (背俞穴) for the Zang-Fu organs — the single most important channel for diagnosing and treating internal organ disharmony from the back
"Tai Yang" — governs the exterior/defensive layer of the body; first channel to be affected by external pathogens (see Shang Han Lun six-stage theory)

acu-points.title: Kidney Channel (KI) — Foot Shao Yin
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts under the little toe, runs diagonally across the sole to KI1, emerges at the tuberosity of the navicular bone, passes behind the medial malleolus, ascends the medial leg (behind SP), through the popliteal fossa, up the medial thigh, enters the spine, connects with the Kidney and Bladder. A branch runs from the Kidney upward through the Liver and diaphragm into the Lung, along the throat to the root of the tongue; another branch leaves the Lung, joins the Heart, and pours into the chest, connecting to the Pericardium channel. The external branch runs from the abdomen (0.5 cun lateral to the midline) up the chest (2 cun lateral) to below the clavicle (KI27).

items.t: Foot Shao Yin, paired (exterior-interior) with the Bladder channel
27 points; KI1 is the only point on the sole of any of the 12 channels
Governs storage of essence, reproduction/growth/development, and reception of Qi — the "root of pre-heaven"; heavily used for constitutional/chronic deficiency patterns

acu-points.title: Sanjiao Channel (SJ/TE) — Hand Shao Yang
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the ulnar side of the ring finger tip (TE1), runs up the dorsum of the hand, between the radius and ulna, over the olecranon, up the posterior-lateral upper arm to the shoulder, crosses over GB21, enters the supraclavicular fossa, spreads through the chest, connects with the Pericardium, descends through the diaphragm, linking the Upper, Middle, and Lower Jiao. A branch runs from the chest up the neck, around the ear, to the outer canthus (TE23), connecting to the Gallbladder channel.

items.t: Hand Shao Yang, paired (exterior-interior) with the Pericardium channel
23 points; runs along the middle of the posterior forearm/arm, between the LI and SI channels

The Sanjiao (Triple Energizer/Burner) governs the passage of Qi and fluids through the Upper/Middle/Lower Jiao; the channel is heavily used for ear disorders, lateral headache, and as a general Shao Yang (exterior-interior transitional) channel alongside GB

acu-points.title: Gallbladder Channel (GB) — Foot Shao Yang
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the outer canthus (GB1), zigzags around the ear and temple, down to GB20 at the base of the skull, crosses the shoulder (GB21), enters the supraclavicular fossa, descends through the chest and diaphragm, connects with the Liver and Gallbladder, then runs along the side of the body (ribs, hip) down the lateral thigh and leg (in front of the BL channel and behind the ST channel), crosses the lateral malleolus, along the dorsum of the foot, ending at the lateral side of the 4th toe (GB44). A branch leaves the dorsum of the foot to the big toe, connecting to the Liver channel.

items.t: Foot Shao Yang, paired (exterior-interior) with the Liver channel
44 points — the second-largest channel; famous for its zigzagging path across the side of the head
Governs decisiveness (together with LV); classically important for headache (especially migraine/one-sided), hypochondriac pain, and — via GB34 — sinews/tendons throughout the body

acu-points.title: Liver Channel (LV) — Foot Jue Yin
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Channel pathway
blocks.lead: Starts at the lateral side of the big toe (LV1), runs across the dorsum of the foot, in front of the medial malleolus, crosses SP6 (crossing above the ankle), up the medial leg (behind SP below the malleolus, then in front above it), through the medial knee and thigh, around the genitals, into the lower abdomen, curves around the Stomach, enters the Liver and connects with the Gallbladder, continues up through the diaphragm, spreads through the hypochondriac region, ascends along the throat, connects with the eye system, emerges at the forehead, and meets Du Mai at the vertex. Branches connect to the cheek/lips and, from the Liver, through the diaphragm to the Lung, linking to the Lung channel and completing the 12-channel cycle.

items.t: Foot Jue Yin, paired (exterior-interior) with the Gallbladder channel
14 points — the last of the 12 primary channels, completing the cycle back to the Lung

Governs the smooth flow of Qi and storage of Blood; LV3 (Taichong) is one of the most important points in the body, especially paired with LI4 as the "Four Gates" (四關) for regulating Qi and calming the spirit

acu-points.title: Conception Vessel (Ren Mai, CV/RE)
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Pathway & role
blocks.lead: Runs straight up the anterior midline of the body, from the perineum (CV1) to the chin (CV24). Together with Du Mai, it is one of the only two Extraordinary Vessels with its own dedicated points; Ren + Du + the 12 primary channels form the "14 channels" (十四經) that carry the great majority of acupuncture points. Ren Mai is described as the "Sea of the Yin channels" (陰脈之海) — it connects with and regulates all the Yin channels of the body, and is closely tied to menstruation, pregnancy, and conception (hence the name).

acu-points.title: Governing Vessel (Du Mai, GV/DU)
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM point location/indication data

blocks.heading: Pathway & role
blocks.lead: Runs up the posterior midline of the body, from the tip of the coccyx (GV1), along the spine, over the top of the head, down the forehead and nose, ending inside the upper lip (GV28). Together with Ren Mai, it is one of the only two Extraordinary Vessels with its own dedicated points. Du Mai is described as the "Sea of the Yang channels" (陽脈之海) — it connects with and regulates all the Yang channels, and is closely related to the Kidney, the brain, and the spinal marrow.

acu-points.title: The Other Six Extraordinary Vessels (Overview)
acu-points.source: Compiled from standard TCM channel theory

blocks.heading: Why these six have no points of their own
blocks.lead: Of the Eight Extraordinary Vessels, only Ren Mai and Du Mai have dedicated points (covered in their own chapters). The remaining six borrow points from the 12 primary channels and from Ren/Du. Each is accessed clinically through its Confluent (Master) point, usually paired with a Coupled point on the opposite pair of limbs (see the Point Categories chapter for the full pairing table).

items.detail: Arises in the lower abdomen (near KI11–21/the uterus), emerges at the perineum, ascends with the Kidney channel alongside the abdomen to the chest and throat, and curves around the mouth; a branch runs down the leg along with the Kidney channel to the foot.
"Sea of the 12 channels" (十二經之海) and "Sea of Blood" (血海) — regulates the Qi and Blood of all 12 primary channels; closely tied to menstruation, fertility, and pregnancy.
SP4 (Gongsun), coupled with PC6 — used together for chest, heart, and stomach disorders.
The only channel that runs horizontally — it circles the waist like a belt, starting below the free end of the 11th rib (near LV13), crossing GB26/27/28.
Binds and holds together all the vertically-running channels; governs leukorrhea and is used for disorders felt as a "band" or "girdle" around the waist.
GB41 (Zulinqi), coupled with TE5 — used together for outer canthus, ear, cheek, neck and shoulder disorders.

blocks.heading: Yang Qiao Mai (陽蹻脈) — Yang Motility Vessel

items.detail: Starts at the lateral side of the heel (BL62), ascends along the lateral leg and trunk, over the shoulder, up the neck, to the outer canthus (meeting the Yin Qiao Mai), and into the brain.
Governs movement of the lateral/Yang side of the body and the opening of the eyes; paired functionally with Yin Qiao Mai to control eyelid opening/closing and leg movement.
BL62 (Shenmai), coupled with SI3 — used together for inner canthus, neck, ear, shoulder, and back disorders.

blocks.heading: Yin Qiao Mai (陰蹻脈) — Yin Motility Vessel

items.detail: Starts at KI6 (medial side of the heel), ascends along the medial leg and trunk to the throat, and joins the Yang Qiao Mai at the inner canthus.
Governs movement of the medial/Yin side of the body; together with Yang Qiao Mai, controls sleep-wake cycles (eyelid opening/closing) — clinically important for insomnia.
KI6 (Zhaohai), coupled with LU7 — used together for lung, throat, chest, and diaphragm disorders.

blocks.heading: Yang Wei Mai (陽維脈) — Yang Linking Vessel

items.detail: Starts at BL63, ascends along the lateral leg, trunk, and shoulder, crosses the posterior neck (meeting Du Mai at GV16), and up to the forehead.
"Links" and connects all the Yang channels of the body; governs the exterior of the body — disorders present as chills/fever (exterior-type patterns).
TE5 (Waiguan), coupled with GB41 — used together for outer canthus, ear, cheek, neck, shoulder disorders.

blocks.heading: Yin Wei Mai (陰維脈) — Yin Linking Vessel

items.detail: Starts at KI9, ascends along the medial leg and trunk, joining the abdomen and chest, meeting Ren Mai at the neck (CV22/23).
"Links" and connects all the Yin channels of the body; governs the interior of the body — disorders present as chest/heart pain (interior-type patterns).
PC6 (Neiguan), coupled with SP4 — used together for chest, heart, and stomach disorders.

blocks.heading: Clinical summary — the Eight Confluent points at a glance

acu-points.title: Point by Region
acu-points.source: Reorganization of the 361-point dataset (Channels chapters) by body region

blocks.heading: How this chapter works
blocks.lead: The same 361 points already covered channel-by-channel, re-sorted here by body region instead — useful for planning a treatment by area (e.g. "what points are available around the knee") rather than by channel. ★ marks points already flagged as high-yield in their channel chapter.

Medial side of the leg, in the depression below the medial condyle of the tibia
Regulates and cools Blood; nourishes Blood
2 cun above the medial border of the patella, on the bulge of vastus medialis
Medial thigh, on the line joining SP10 and SP12, 6 cun above SP10
Anterior thigh, level with the perineum, lateral to sartorius
6 cun above the lateral border of the patella, on the line from ASIS to the lateral patella
3 cun above the lateral border of the patella
Regulates Stomach Qi; alleviates acute pain (Xi-cleft — acute gastric pain)
2 cun above the lateral border of the patella
Expels Wind-damp; benefits the knee joint
Lateral depression of the patella (lateral "eye" of the knee), knee flexed
Tonifies Qi and Blood; strengthens the Spleen/Stomach; strengthens the body's defenses (major tonification point)
3 cun below ST35, one finger-breadth (middle finger) lateral to the anterior tibial crest
Regulates the Large Intestine; resolves damp-heat
6 cun below ST35, one finger-breadth lateral to the tibial crest
Regulates the Stomach and Intestines; relaxes sinews; benefits the shoulder (distal)
Regulates the Small Intestine; resolves damp-heat
Resolves phlegm and damp; calms the Shen (key phlegm point)
8 cun above the lateral malleolus, ~2 finger-breadths lateral to ST38, midway between ST35 and the lateral malleolus
Clears Stomach heat; calms the Shen; benefits the ankle joint
Midpoint of the ankle crease, between the tendons of extensor hallucis longus and extensor digitorum longus
Dorsum of the foot, at the highest point, where the dorsalis pedis artery pulsates
Dorsum of foot, depression proximal to the 2nd/3rd metatarsophalangeal joints
Clears Stomach heat and Fire; benefits the Intestines
Between the 2nd and 3rd toes, proximal to the margin of the web
Clears Stomach heat; calms the Shen
Lateral side of the 2nd toe, 0.1 cun proximal to the corner of the nail

blocks.heading: 4 Diagnostics (四診)
blocks.lead: Diagnostic methods used in TCM - process of collecting information about the disease(s)

items.detail: spirit, complexion, demeanor, tongue diagnosis, ...
questions about the condition(s)
pulse diagnosis, palpation (chest, abdomen, acupuncture points, etc.)

blocks.heading: Characteristics of TCM diagnosis / pattern differentiation

items.t: Manifestations not considered signs/symptoms in Western biomedical science may be considered important in TCM
items.detail: e.g. thirst, glitter in the eyes, ...

items.t: "Through observing the outward manifestations, [one] knows the internal organs and in turn, the location of the disease"
items.cn: 視其外應, 以知其內藏, 則知所病矣
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 47 (靈樞: 本臟)>

items.t: Collected signs and symptoms must be considered in relation to others
items.detail: e.g. thirst + yellow tongue coating + rapid pulse → excess heat; thirst with no coating + thin pulse → deficiency heat

items.t: Pattern differentiation should be made based on all information gathered by the 4 diagnostics

blocks.heading: Looking Diagnostics (Wang Zhen, 望診)
blocks.lead: Observing the physical and mental aspects of the patient

items.t: Looking the Head, Neck, 5 Sensory Organs, and 9 Orifices
Observing vitality, mental/emotional state, eyes, facial expression, facial complexion, behavior, breathing, speaking (degree of clarity), etc. in a collective manner
Presence of the spirit, loss of the spirit, or false spirit

items.detail: Broad meaning: outward expression of the life process = vitality. Narrow meaning: mental and emotional activities.

The spirit cannot exist without a physical body, and life cannot exist without the spirit. "[One who] loses spirit dies, [and one who] obtains spirit lives (失神者死, 得神者生也)" <Ling Shu: Chapter 54 (靈樞: 千年)>. Physical body and spirit have a mutual relationship: physical health/disease ↔ spirit; food, exercise, rest → health/disorder of body → health/disorder of spirit → emotional disturbance, stress.

"Life's origin is called the essence. Mutual interaction of both essences is called spirit (生之來謂之精, 兩精相博謂之神)" <Ling Shu: Chapter 8 (靈樞: 本神)>; "Thus, spirit is the essence of the food stuff (故神者, 水穀之精氣也)" <Ling Shu: Chapter 32 (靈樞: 平人絶穀)>. Pre-heaven and post-heaven essence → spirit; essence produces spirit and spirit controls essence; Qi produces spirit and spirit controls Qi. When essence is full, Qi is abundant, and the spirit is active → healthy. When essence is deteriorated, Qi is deficient, and the spirit is damaged → cause of disease or aging. Essence → Qi → spirit.

items.t: Healthy facial complexion, glitter in the eyes, full of vitality, clear mind, breathing evenly, clear verbal expression, normal conversation, normal behavior, firm muscle tone
Right Qi (正氣) is still intact
The condition is relatively mild, or it may be improved relatively quickly or easily
Dark (dull) facial complexion, lack of vitality, lack of facial expression, no glitter in the eyes, breathing with difficulty, low and weak voice, fatigued looking, withered muscle tone, ...
The condition is relatively severe, or it may be worsened relatively quickly or easily
Exhibition of a false state of vitality, usually occurring in critically ill patients
Usually loss of the spirit + sudden vitality, increased appetite, flashes of red complexion on the cheeks, clear speaking, etc., as if the person is recovering
Extremely critical state before getting even worse → separation of Yin and Yang
False signs may be incorrectly identified as improvement
"The last radiance of the setting sun"

items.detail: Mild type of "loss of the spirit". Sleepy, fatigued, low and weak voice, forgetful → deficiency (HT/SP, KI Yang)
Restlessness, agitation, delirious speech, uncontrolled laughter or crying, depression, speaking to oneself → excess (fire, phlegm, blood stasis), deficiency (HT/SP)
Sudden loss of consciousness with uncontrolled salivation, spasmodic limbs, becoming normal again after each episode → LV wind, phlegm blocking the clear orifices, phlegm-fire, wind-phlegm

items.t: "12 channels and 365 collaterals, their Qi and blood all go up to the face and run into the orifices."
items.cn: 十二經脈, 三百六十五絡, 其血氣皆上於面而走空竅
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 4 (靈樞: 邪氣藏府病形)>

items.t: "Generally, essence, glitter, and 5 colors are the manifestation of Qi."
items.cn: 夫精明五色者, 氣之華也
items.detail: <Su Wen: Chapter 17 (素問: 脈要精微論)>

items.t: The state of essence, Qi, and blood produced by the 5 Zang organs is reflected on the face
Observing the color, luster, and skin lesions of the face

blocks.heading: Face and 5 Zang organs — heat disease areas
blocks.lead: <Su Wen: Chapter 32 (素問: 刺熱論)>

items.t: "One with LV heat disease, the left cheek area becomes red first. One with HT heat disease, the forehead becomes red first. One with SP heat disease, the nose becomes red first. One with LU heat disease, the right cheek area becomes red first. One with KI heat disease, the chin becomes red first. Although a disease has not occurred, [if one] observes the red color, [one] may needle. This is called treating Wei Bing."
items.cn: 肝熱病者, 左頰先赤; 心熱病者, 顔先赤; 脾熱病者, 鼻先赤; 肺熱病者, 右頰先赤; 腎熱病者, 頤先赤. 病雖未發, 見赤色者刺之, 名曰治未病
items.detail: <Su Wen: Chapter 32 (素問: 刺熱論)>

blocks.heading: Face and 5 Zang organs — Ling Shu chapter 49 map
blocks.lead: <Ling Shu: Chapter 49 (靈樞: 五色)>

items.t: Between the eyebrows (glabella area) - throat, LU, and HT
Area above the apex of the nose - SI
Area below the apex of the nose - BL, "child place"
1) Ways of observing the facial complexion
items.detail: First, observe the location where any abnormal color appears; then observe the color and quality that appear on the location; finally, analyze the location, color, and quality in an integrated manner
Must have clear color (without any other abnormal colors), proper moisture (not dry), and full of luster (not dull)

items.t: The nose must have proper moisture, luster, and clear color
3) Clear (or shining) color vs. dull (or dry) color
items.detail: Clear + moist + luster → intact ST Qi → positive prognosis even if pathogenic color present. Dull-dry color + lack of moisture and luster → exhausted ST Qi → negative prognosis

"When observing the location of the 5 colors, observe its floating and sinking to know shallow and deep; observe its sparse and dense to see success and defeat; observe its scattering and collecting to know the far and near; look at the color's up and down to know the location of disease." (五色各見其部, 察其浮沉, 以知淺深; 察其澤夭, 以觀成敗; 察其散搏, 以知遠近; 視色上下, 以知病處) <Ling Shu: Chapter 49>. "Deep-turbid is the interior; floating-sparse is the exterior." (沉濁爲內, 浮澤爲外) <Ling Shu: Chapter 49>

blocks.heading: Pairs used to interpret color quality

rows: Right Qi deficiency vs. replete evil Qi
New or resolving vs. chronic or stagnated

blocks.heading: 5 colors and facial complexion
blocks.lead: <Ling Shu: Chapter 49 (靈樞: 五色)>: "Blue, black are pain; yellow, red are heat; white is cold (靑黑爲痛, 黃赤爲熱, 白爲寒)." "Yellow, red are wind; blue, black is pain; white is cold (黃赤爲風, 靑黑爲痛, 白爲寒)."

items.detail: Cold, pain, blood stasis, internal wind, LV. [Note] Purplish (紫): more severe than blue
Heat, deserting Yang (戴陽)
Damp, deficiency, SP, heat
Deficiency, cold, blood loss (脫血), blood deficiency, deserting Qi (奪氣)
Cold, pain, KI deficiency, water-fluids, blood stasis

items.t: Observing the whole body + specific body parts

items.detail: Properly developed chest, abundant flesh with strength, moist skin with luster. Strong Zangfu organs, full of Qi and blood
Under- or abnormally-developed chest (chicken chest, turtle back), emaciated, dry-dull-coarse skin. Weak Zangfu organs, Qi and blood insufficiency
Balance between Qi and body shape: relatively weak Qi vs. body shape → unfavorable prognosis; relatively stronger Qi vs. body shape → favorable prognosis
Good appetite → eating too much. Poor appetite → replete appearance but deficient Qi = SP deficiency + retention of damp and phlegm, usually soft flesh with dull complexion of the skin
Good appetite → heat in the middle Jiao. Poor appetite → weak middle Jiao Qi. Dry bones (teeth) + loss of big muscle tone → exhaustion of Zangfu organs' essence

items.t: Balance between Yin and Yang in general
items.detail: Relatively stronger Yang (relative Yin ↓, relative Yang ↑) → tend to be skinny. Relatively stronger Yin (relative Yang ↓, relative Yin ↑) → tend to be obese
Sinews (weak or stiff) → LV; blood vessels (hardening) → HT; flesh (flaccid or tight) → SP; skin (flaccid or withered) → LU; bones (brittle) → KI

Excessive movement of the limbs, restlessness, agitation, speaking a lot, manic behavior, preference to uncover oneself with a blanket, changing position restlessly while lying in bed → Yang, excess, heat. Lack of movement, preference for curled position, dislike to talk, heavy sensation of the body → Yin, deficiency, cold

Twitching of the facial muscle or eyelids (tics), shaking (tremor) of the head or limbs → internal LV wind. Difficulty bending the joints, stiffness of the limbs, swollen joints, deformation of joints → Bi syndrome (painful obstruction, 痹證). Emaciation of limbs, difficulty grabbing objects → Wei syndrome (flaccid syndrome, 痿證). Dizziness worse with sitting and better with lying down → phlegm. Severe conditions → trying to grab the air (space), rubbing the bed or own clothes

items.t: Preferring sitting & prone (face down) position when lying down → LU deficiency with insufficiency of Qi
Preferring sitting & supine (face up) position when lying down → LU excess + rebellious Qi
Preferring sitting and unable to lie down → asthma, distention of LU, retention of water-fluids in the chest

acu-diag.title: Looking the Head, Neck, 5 Sensory Organs & 9 Orifices

blocks.heading: Looking the Head, Neck, 5 Sensory Organs, and 9 Orifices

items.t: "12 channels and 365 collaterals, their Qi and blood all go up to the face and run into the orifices."
items.cn: 十二經脈, 三百六十五絡, 其血氣皆上於面而走空竅
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 4 (靈樞: 邪氣藏府病形)>

blocks.heading: Head, face, and hair (頭, 面, 頭髮)

items.t: 1) Characteristics of the head, face, and hair
items.detail: Head = place where all Yang channels meet: hand & foot Yang channels + DU + (LV & RE). KI controls the marrow and manifests on the hair: head = storage of the brain (sea of marrow) and marrow; hair = outward manifestation of KI. HT controls the blood vessels and its manifestation shows in the complexion. KI & HT play important roles in physiology and pathology of the head, face, and hair

Abnormally large head → damaged KI essence, pre-heaven insufficiency, fluid retention. Sunken fontanel (囟陷) → deficiency (slightly depressed up to 6 months usually normal); causes: frequent vomiting/diarrhea, Qi and blood deficiency, SP/ST empty-cold, deficiency of pre-heaven essence. Raised (bulging/tense) fontanel (囟塡) → excess heat: evil Qi invasion (warm diseases, wind-heat, damp-heat), fluid retention. Unclosed fontanel with difficulty erecting the neck → KI deficiency, malnutrition

items.t: Shaking of the head without control → wind, Qi and blood deficiency
Swelling of the face → water retention, heat-toxin (swollen-red)
items.detail: Yang type water retention (陽水): usually first from eyelids/head/face, then to lower limbs and abdomen; no dipping or relatively quick recovery from dipping. Yin type water retention (陰水): usually first from lower limbs/abdomen, then to face/head; slow recovery from dipping

items.t: Deviated mouth and eyes + reduced or no sensation of the face → wind

items.detail: Moist, shiny, deep (thick) color + dense quality → health (full of KI, essence, blood). Dry and dull color + hair loss (entire head) → deficiency (essence, blood, KI). Sudden hair loss in young age → blood deficiency + wind. General hair loss in young age → KI deficiency, blood-heat, blood deficiency. Premature graying of hair → deficiency (KI, blood)

items.t: "Zangfu organs' essence and Qi all flow up into the eyes and become essence (the eyes are able to function properly)."
items.cn: 五臟六腑之精氣, 皆上注於目而爲之精
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 80 (靈樞: 大惑論)> → place to observe the state of internal organs

items.t: "The container of essence is the eyes. Essence of the bone becomes pupil. Essence of the sinews becomes iris. Essence of blood becomes collaterals. Essence of Qi becomes sclera. Essence of the flesh becomes eyelids."
items.cn: 精之窠爲眼, 骨之精爲瞳子, 筋之精爲黑眼, 血之精爲絡, 其窠氣之精爲白眼, 肌肉之精爲約束, 裹擷筋骨血氣之精
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 80 (靈樞: 大惑論)>

rows: Lateral & medial corners of the eye (blood wheel, 血輪)
Upper and lower eyelids (flesh wheel, 肉輪)

items.t: Eyes with proper vision, moisture, glitter + clear boundary between sclera and iris → health, good prognosis in diseases
Eyes without glitter, no desire to open, or staring at one direction, poor vision → bad prognosis

items.detail: Color change on sclera → red (HT), white (LU), blue (LV), yellow (SP), black (KI). Red on medial/lateral canthus → HT fire. Red on sclera → LU heat, wind-heat. Yellow on sclera → damp. Red-swollen eyelids with pus (sty) → SP fire. Entire eyes red → heat in LV channel
Clear eyes → cold. Turbid-dark → heat
Bright → phlegm-fluids. Dark-dull → KI deficiency

items.t: Crossed eyes, unable to move the eyes, looking upwards without control → LV wind

items.detail: Sunken → declined Zangfu organs' essence (critical), worse if unable to see things. Raised (slightly bulged) → initial stage of water retention

2 types: blockage at the iris (翳), blockage at the sclera (膜). Red, swollen, itchy, painful, dry → mostly excess; external evil invasions (六淫), retention of food, phlegm-fire, damp-heat, Qi stagnation (emotional stress) damaging Zangfu organs. [Ex] Conjunctivitis (pink eyes)

Generally no abnormal findings with the naked eye (may notice some color/shape change) + vision problems → mostly deficiency; Qi and blood deficiency from Qi stagnation (emotional stress), empty-heat from LV/KI deficiency, heat accumulation from external evil invasion. [Ex] Glaucoma (綠內障), cataract (白內障)
LU opens into the nose; hand & foot Yangming channels

items.t: "Is 5-color determined only at Ming Tang? Ming Tang is the nose. The bone of Ming Tang should rise high and be straight. 5-Zang organs follow the middle, and 6-Fu organs are placed on both sides."
items.cn: 五色獨決于明堂乎? 明堂者鼻也. 明堂骨高以起, 平以直, 五藏次于中央, 六府挾其兩側
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 49 (靈樞: 五色)>

items.t: "[If] the color on the apex of the nose is blue, [it indicates] abdominal pain; [if] painful and cold, [one] dies. [If] slightly black, there is water Qi. [If] yellow, there is cold above the chest. [If] white, it is collapse of blood. [If] slight red appears at improper time, [one] dies."
items.cn: 鼻頭色靑, 腹中痛, 苦冷者死; 鼻頭色微黑者, 有水氣; 色黃者, 胸上有寒; 色白者, 亡血也, 設微赤非時者死
items.detail: <Jin Gui Yao Lue: Chapter 1 (金匱要略: 臟腑經絡先後病脈證)>
Clear color + moist + luster → health or good prognosis even in disease

items.t: Color on the apex of the nose
items.detail: Blue → abdominal pain. Yellow → damp(-heat). White → blood loss. Red → heat in SP & LU. Black (gray) → water Qi. Yellowish-black + withered → SP fire + dried up body fluids
Dry → Yangming heat. Dry-black → Yang toxin (toxic heat). Cold-slippery-black → Yin toxin (toxic cold)
Swollen and bulged → replete evil Qi. Red-swollen + skin lesions → blood heat. Flaring and dry nostrils → LU heat
Clear + watery → cold. Yellowish + sticky → heat
KI opens into the ears; hand & foot Shaoyang + hand Taiyang + foot Yangming channels

Proper amount of flesh & moisture + luster + pinkish-red → health. Thin, withered, pale → KI deficiency. White → cold; defeated KI (critical) if shallow-white. Black → pain; KI Yin deficiency if black + dry-withered auricle. Red → heat if red-swollen

Swollen → excess (heat). Emaciated → deficiency (Qi, KI). Dry & scaling auricle → blood stasis, intestinal abscess. Suppurative excretion + smell (膿耳) → wind-heat in LV/GB channels, damp-heat in LV/GB, empty-heat from KI Yin deficiency
SP opens into the mouth and manifests on the lips; ST channel

Slightly pale-red + moist + luster → health. Pale-red → deficiency (blood and/or Qi), cold. Pale-white → exhaustion of blood, loss of blood. Dark-red → excess-heat (+dry → heat damaging body fluids; +dry+swollen → extreme heat). Blue or black → cold, pain, blood stasis (pale-red+black → severe cold; blue-black → extreme cold; blue → Qi and blood stagnation; purplish-blue → stagnated heat; black around the mouth → KI exhaustion, critical; purplish-black + dry mouth/lips → critical)

items.t: Shape and quality of the mouth and lips
items.detail: Dry and cracked → body fluids damage (external dry-heat, SP heat, Yin deficiency). Drooling from one corner of the mouth → SP deficiency with damp, ST heat, stroke. Canker sore → HT & SP channel heat (empty if pale-red; excess if bright-red + festered). Eroded mouth → empty-heat, SP channel damp-heat. Critical: shrunken philtrum & lips, unable to close the mouth (SP or LU)
Teeth = extension of the bone which is controlled by KI. Gums = hand and foot Yangming channels (ST, LI)

Clean-white, luster, moist → health, reflects healthy KI. Yellow-dry → exuberant heat + damaged body fluids. Dry-shiny like stone → excess heat in Yangming. Dry like withered bone → KI Yin exhaustion. Clenched → wind

Pale-white → blood deficiency. Pale & emaciated → ST Yin deficiency, KI Qi deficiency. Swollen-red → ST fire. Swollen-red + bleeding + painful → ST heat damaging collaterals. Swollen + (bleeding) + no pain → Qi deficiency or KI empty-heat damaging collaterals
The throat belongs to LU and is the gate to ST (breathing + food). KI channel runs along the throat and terminates at the root of the tongue

Pinkish-red + moist & slippery + neither swollen/painful + no abnormal sensation on breathing/talking/swallowing → health. Red-swollen and painful → LU and/or ST heat-toxin, empty-fire from KI Yin deficiency (less painful and red). Pale-red + swollen all around → phlegm-damp accumulation. Pale-red + no swelling + slight recurrent pain + dry + itchy → empty-fire uprising from Qi and Yin deficiency. Red-swollen + festered → excess heat, heat-toxin, deficiency (if pale-red)
LU and KI Yin deficiency + empty-heat, wind-heat toxin
LV Qi and phlegm stagnation

items.t: Stiffness or flaccidity of the neck
items.detail: Stiff → excess. Flaccid → deficiency

blocks.heading: Lower orifices (下竅: 前陰, 後陰)

items.detail: External genital (anterior Yin, 前陰) + anus (posterior Yin, 後陰). KI opens into the front and back orifices and controls urination and bowel movement. Front orifice → BL, KI, uterus, SP/ST, LV/GB + RE, DU. Back orifice → LI, LU, SP, ST, KI + RE, DU

Swelling of the testicles: one type of Shan Qi (hernia, 疝氣); swollen + no pain/itch → Yin swelling (陰腫) from wind-damp, water-swelling; commonly due to LV Qi stagnation, cold, damp-heat, Qi deficiency. Swelling of vagina (陰腫): swollen + pain → damage to blood (heat or deficiency); swollen + no pain → water-swelling. Retracted testicles/penis/vagina (陰縮): urgent condition, from cold (freezing the channels) or heat entering Jueyin (severe damage to Yin fluids). [Note] STD → festering skin lesions + urinary problems
Torn anus → LI heat (painful, bleeding during bowel movement). Hemorrhoid → damp-heat, Qi deficiency, ... Prolapsed rectum → Qi sinking

items.t: The skin is the most exterior of the body, and defensive Qi (Wei Qi, 衛氣) flows between the skin → front line defense against external evil Qi invasion
The state of Zangfu, through channels and collaterals, manifests on the skin
Same approach as the facial complexion

items.detail: Red + large + raised (+swelling) → erysipelas (丹毒). More on the upper body → HT fire + wind-heat. More on the lower body → KI fire accumulation, damp-heat downward flow

Yellow skin + yellow sclera/face/nails → jaundice (黃疸). Yang jaundice (陽黃) → LV/GB damp-heat. Yin jaundice (陰黃) → SP/ST damp-cold. [Note] Black jaundice (黑疸, 女勞疸): yellow + dark-dull black (gray) → KI damage

items.t: Withered skin and body hair → Taiyin (LU, SP) deficiency
Loss of body hair or dry skin with breaking of body hair → damaged LU
Dry skin with scales (like fish scales) (肌膚甲錯)
items.detail: Dark-black eyeballs → internal dry blood. Abdominal pain → abscess

blocks.heading: Swelling and distention (Zhong Zhang, 腫脹)

items.t: Flat or bulged supraclavicular fossa (ST12 area), center of the sole (KI1 area), back, or umbilicus + dark-black lips → bad prognosis

items.detail: Swelling (Zhong, 腫; 浮腫): appears on the head and face, chest and abdomen, lower and upper back, and 4 limbs. Distention (Zhang, 脹; 臌脹): distention of the abdomen

items.t: Raised (erupted) → heat, excess. Depressed → cold, deficiency
Red → heat. Pale → deficiency (Qi, blood), wind

items.detail: Red skin lesions (usually from chest/abdomen to 4 limbs), fever. Heat (LU, ST)
Unclear-small pale-red or dark-purplish skin lesions (no fixed location, appearing & disappearing), thin-weak pulse, cold limbs. Qi and blood deficiency

items.t: Measles/rubeola (Ma Zhen, 麻疹) and German measles/rubella (Feng Zhen, 風疹)
items.detail: Red rashes, usually begin with common-cold-like symptoms. External invasion of wind-heat, wind-warm

items.t: Urticaria/hives (Yin Zhen, 隱疹; Xuan Ma Zhen, 蕁麻疹)
items.detail: Pale-red (or bright-red), raised, itchy bumps, burning or stinging sensation, appearing-disappearing. Blood deficiency + wind attacking the channels

items.t: Usually no hot sensation, mild pain
Attacking deeper level than the skin → flesh, sinews
Usually Yin pattern → Qi and blood deficiency + cold stagnation

blocks.heading: Superficial venules of the index finger (3-gate)

items.detail: Used for children under 3 years old. Observing the superficial venules (considered an extension of the branches of the LU channel) appearing on the palmar side of the index finger, on the radius side
Wind-gate (風關): proximal interphalangeal joint, palmar side. Qi-gate (氣關): middle interphalangeal joint, palmar side. Life-gate (命關): distal interphalangeal joint, palmar side
Hold the child's wrist with the left hand; with the right thumb, gently push the child's index finger from the distal end to the palm several times

Visible only at wind-gate → relatively mild condition. Extending to Qi-gate → relatively severe condition. Extending to life-gate → severe condition. Extending to the nail, passing through the 3-gate → critical

items.t: Color, depth, and shape of the venules
items.detail: Superficial + red or yellowish-red, faintly visible or invisible at wind-gate, usually a slightly slanted single venule (length naturally shortens with age; length/width vary with weather — longer when hot, shorter/thinner when cold) → normal. Floating vs. deep venules → exterior vs. interior. Superficial vs. dense color → mild vs. severe. Purplish-red vs. bright-red → internal heat vs. external invasion. Blue → wind, pain. Pale vs. purplish-black → deficiency vs. blood stasis (or emergency). Venules getting longer vs. shorter → worsening vs. recovering. Venules getting thicker vs. thinner → heat/excess vs. cold/deficiency. Single vs. multiple venules → mild vs. severe

blocks.heading: Fish Border (Yu Ji, 魚際) area

items.t: On the palmar side, the flesh around the metacarpal of the thumb (LU10)
Abundant and firm flesh → healthy ST

items.detail: Blue, black → pain, ST cold, deficiency (if venules are short). Red → ST heat

items.t: Nails are the extension of the sinews → LV. LV stores blood → state of Qi and blood shown on the nails

items.detail: Dark-red → heat in Qi level. Yellow → jaundice (damp-heat). Pale-white → blood deficiency, Qi and blood deficiency, cold (SP & KI Yang deficiency). Purplish-black → blood stasis. Blue → cold

items.t: Slow recovery of color after pressing the nail → blood stasis, Qi stagnation
Unable to recover red color → blood deficiency
Flat or indented nails → LV blood deficiency
Withered nails → Bi (painful obstruction, 痹) syndrome

items.detail: Heat boiling body fluids
Cold damaging Yang Qi → unable to transform fluids → accumulation of damp → phlegm
LV wind accompanying phlegm, usually blocking the clear orifices

items.t: White-slippery + large amount + easy to spit out → phlegm-damp (濕痰)
items.detail: SP deficiency → accumulation of water-damp → phlegm

items.t: Sticky + less amount + difficult to spit out → dry-phlegm (燥痰)
items.detail: Dry (autumn) evil damaging LU

items.t: Phlegm with blood → empty-heat from Yin deficiency damaging LU collaterals
items.detail: Phlegm with blood + pus + smell → LU abscess (肺癰)

items.t: Cough + vomiting saliva + open mouth + shortness of breath → LU atrophy (肺痿)
Chronic turbid-profuse nasal discharge → sinusitis (鼻淵)
Drooling without acknowledging → SP Qi deficiency unable to hold, or ST heat with parasite accumulation in infants
Frequent-profuse salivation → cold (ST, KI), damp, food stagnation

items.detail: Tuo (唾) - KI; Xian (涎) - SP

items.t: Vomiting = rebellious (moving against normal course of Qi flow) ST Qi due to various reasons
Clear-watery + no smell → cold (ST cold, SP/KI Yang deficiency)
Turbid + smell → heat (ST heat, LV attacking ST)
Undigested food → indigestion (foul smell), LV attacking ST (usually no smell)
Water and/or slippery phlegm + no thirst + greasy tongue coating → phlegm-fluids
Yellow-bitter fluids → LV/GB damp-heat attacking ST
Vomitus with blood or purplish blood clots → ST heat, LV fire attacking ST, blood stasis
Conditions observed from the tongue body and coating must be interpreted in combination with other manifestations
1) Observing the state of Qi and blood (正氣盛衰)
items.detail: "Tongue (body) is the outward manifestation of HT, and its coating is the evidence of ST. Observing the tongue (body) reveals repletion or decline of the right Qi, and observing its coating shows entrance or exit of evil Qi." Healthy tongue — body: pinkish-red + moist → replete Qi and blood; coating: thin-white + moist → replete ST Qi. [Ex] Pale-white tongue → Qi and blood deficiency; no coating → ST deficiency (Qi, Yin)

items.t: 2) Observing the depth (exterior or interior) of evil Qi
items.detail: "Differentiating the tongue body determines the 5 Zang organs' excess and deficiency. Observing the tongue coating reveals the shallow and deep of the 6 evil Qi." Thickness of the tongue coating → depth and severity of pathogenic invasion. [Ex] Thin coating → early stage of disease, exterior (relatively shallow level). [Ex] Thick coating → interior (relatively deep level)

items.t: 3) Observing the characteristics (heat, cold, damp, etc.) of evil Qi
items.detail: Each evil Qi has different characteristics reflected on the tongue: yellow coating → heat; white-slippery coating → cold; rotten-greasy coating → retention of food, turbid phlegm; yellow-thick-greasy coating → damp-heat; deviated tongue → wind; dark spots/patches on the tongue → blood stasis

There may be changes in the color/quality of the tongue body and/or coating during a disease process. White → yellow → grey-black coating: exterior to interior, mild to severe, cold to heat. Moist → dry: heat damaging body fluids. Thick → thin coating and dry → moist: retreating evil Qi and recovery of body fluids

items.t: 1) Relationship between Zangfu and the tongue
items.detail: Zangfu organs through channels are directly and indirectly related to the tongue; among these, HT, SP, & ST are most closely related to the tongue

items.t: HT opens into the tongue and controls blood
items.detail: → tongue body (color, appearance, movement) + taste + appetite + speech

items.t: Transforming/transporting of SP and receiving of ST
items.detail: → tongue coating + taste + appetite

items.t: 2) Location of Zangfu on the tongue — according to ST
items.detail: Used when diagnosing ST disorders. Tip of the tongue (舌尖) → upper part of ST. Middle portion (舌中) → middle part of ST. Root of the tongue (舌根) → lower part of ST
Tip of the tongue (舌尖) → HT, LU. Middle portion (舌中) → SP, ST. Sides of the middle portion (舌邊) → LV (left), GB (right). Root of the tongue (舌根) → KI

items.t: Some factors may change the color and/or quality of the tongue
items.detail: Food intake may taint the color of the tongue coating. Depending on season, time of day, or age of the patient, the tongue may change

items.t: Observe the tongue under natural light, without any shadow, if possible
Ask the patient to stick out the tongue
items.detail: Gently (without force) and long enough to examine the entire tongue. Prolonged protruding may change the quality of the tongue (take a break and re-examine)

items.t: Observe first the coating (thickness, color, dry-moist, ...), then the body (color, texture, enlarged-emaciated, blood stasis spots, short-long, ...)

items.detail: Basically pinkish-red tongue body with thin-white coating; other qualities: enough moist, neither stiff nor flaccid (flexible, free of movement, enough strength), no abnormal shape or lesions

items.t: Normal → pinkish-red, moist, proper movement (flexible, neither stiff nor flaccid), proper size (neither enlarged nor thin), no lesions
Color + quality + movement → presence or absence of the spirit of the tongue
Pinkish-red + moist + glitter + proper movement → spirit present → health, good prognosis
Dull color, dry, no glitter, lack of vitality → no spirit → disease, bad prognosis

blocks.heading: Color of the tongue body (舌質色)
blocks.lead: Normal color = pinkish-red (淡紅). Spectrum: (no color of blood) → pale → [pinkish-red: normal] → red → deep red

items.detail: Colors ranging from relatively shallow-pale to completely pale-white (no color of blood). Insufficient Yang → declined production of blood + lack of blood-moving strength → unable to nourish the tongue. Deficiency (Yang, Qi & blood), cold. Pale-white with enlarged body + slippery (wet) coating → empty-cold from Yang deficiency. Pale-white-shiny + emaciated body → Qi and blood deficiency

Relatively deep-red or bright-red compared to normal (pinkish-red). Heat (excess, deficiency): heat in blood → reckless/forceful circulation of Qi and blood → tongue congested by blood. Bright-red + thick-yellow coating (+tongue bumps) → excess heat. Bright-red + scanty or no coating (+fissures) → empty-heat from Yin deficiency

Stronger red, darker than the red tongue. Heat (external, internal), blood stasis. Deep-red or deep-red spots + tongue bumps → febrile disease (heat entering Ying blood level). Deep-red + scanty or no coating (+fissures) → empty-fire from Yin deficiency. Deep-red + scanty coating + moist → blood stasis

Purplish tongue — compared to blue, still some redness. Heat, cold. Purple + dry → heat damaging body fluids + Qi and blood stagnation. Purple (or pale-purplish, purplish-blue) + moist → cold + blood stasis

Blue with lack of red color, similar to bluish vessels on the skin. Exuberant cold evil oppressing Yang, blood stasis (exuberant Yin cold evil → oppressed Yang → sluggish movement of blood → blood stasis). Entire tongue blue → direct cold-strike into LV/KI causing oppressed Yang. Blue on the side(s) of the tongue + dry mouth (no desire to drink, only rinse mouth) → blood stasis
Rough texture and solid-looking tongue body → excess. Delicate texture and soft-looking tongue body → deficiency

Bigger and thicker (as if swollen) than normal. Water-damp or phlegm-fluids retention. Enlarged-pale-white-soft body + slippery coating → SP & KI Yang deficiency → unable to transform body fluids → retention/accumulation of water-fluids. Enlarged & pinkish-red or red body + yellow-greasy coating → SP & ST damp-heat + phlegm-fluids. [Note] Swollen-distended tongue (腫脹): unable to retract or close the mouth if severe → HT/SP heat, heat toxin (drinking, febrile disease), or poisoning
Thinner and smaller than normal. Deficiency (Qi, blood, Yin). Thin + pale → Qi and blood deficiency. Thin-small + deep-red + dry → empty-fire from Yin deficiency + body fluids damage

items.t: 3) Stasis spots and tongue bumps (瘀斑, 芒刺)
items.detail: Tongue bumps → excess heat (heat-toxin, febrile diseases, damp-heat) regardless of color. Stasis spots → heat-toxin (regardless of color), blood stasis

Horizontal or vertical cracks. Deficiency (Yin, blood, Qi). Deep-red + fissures → Yin deficiency (heat damaging Yin). Pale-white + fissures → blood deficiency (unable to nourish the tongue). Pale-white + fissures + enlarged + teeth marks → SP deficiency + damp

Shiny like the surface of a mirror, without coating. Exhaustion of ST Yin, severe damage of ST Qi — indicates severe damage of ST regardless of tongue color. Glossy + pale-white → SP & ST damage, exhaustion of Qi and blood. Glossy + deep-red → fire exhausting ST/KI Yin fluids

Indented marks on the sides of the tongue, usually with enlarged tongue. SP deficiency + damp (SP deficiency → unable to transform/transport → retention of water-damp). Teeth marks + pale-white + slippery coating → cold-damp. Teeth marks + pinkish-red → SP or Qi deficiency

Congested tongue (重舌): congested-swollen sublingual veins as if there is an extra small tongue → HT fire. Bleeding tongue (舌衄): heat (HT, ST, or LV), SP deficiency (unable to hold blood), deserting Yang. Lesions on the tongue: eruptions (usually painful), erosions (usually no pain, Yin deficiency), mostly heat/fire/toxin — lesions on top → HT, SP; lesions on bottom → KI, SP. Sublingual blood vessels: location of extra points Jinjin (金津) & Yuye (玉液); blue-purplish or purplish-black eruption/congestion/swelling → blood stasis

Stiff and rigid, movement difficult, with speaking difficulty due to stiffness. Deep-red body → excess heat (febrile disease entering PC and damaging body fluids). Pinkish-red or purplish-blue body → wind stroke (LV wind + phlegm, or LV Yang rising). Enlarged body + thick-greasy coating → turbid-phlegm. [Note] Numbed (麻痹舌): numbed sensation & movement → LV wind due to blood deficiency, wind-phlegm

Too weak to hold or move. Pale body + chronic condition → Qi & blood deficiency. Red body + dry coating + acute condition → heat damaging body fluids. Deep-red body + chronic condition → severely damaged Yin
Shaking without control. Slight shaking in chronic condition → deficiency (Qi and blood, Yang). Shaking like a hand-held fan in external heat diseases → wind (by extreme heat), alcoholic
Deviated to one side when trying to stick it out. Wind (phlegm). [Note] Disease in the left or right side → tongue deviated to right or left side, respectively

Protruded (吐舌): tongue protruded outside the mouth and cannot retract. Fidgeting (弄舌): unable to steadily protrude the tongue, retracted back without control. Heat in HT & SP (HT-heat → wind; SP-heat → damages body fluids). [Note] Difference from swollen-distended (腫脹) tongue

Very short tongue → critical condition. Pale-white or blue-purplish + slippery coating → cold. Enlarged + greasy coating → phlegm. Deep-red + dry coating → wind induced by extreme heat. Pale-white & enlarged body → Qi & blood deficiency

blocks.heading: Tongue Coating (舌苔) — color
blocks.lead: Observe color and quality (thickness, distribution, dry-moist, ...). Normal = thin-white, moist

items.detail: Exterior, cold. Thin-white coating → exterior, normal. Pale tongue + white-slippery coating → interior cold, cold-damp. [Note] Special case of "heat": thick-white (as if build-up of white powder; usually febrile disease or internal abscess); white-dry coating with fissures (before changing to yellow)

Heat, interior. Density (light, deep, or burnt) of yellow → severity of heat. Thin-light yellow → wind-heat, external evil entering the interior (Yangming, Qi level). Pale-enlarged body + yellow-slippery coating → Yang deficiency + water-damp
Interior (heat, cold). Grey-dry → exuberant heat damaging body fluids, empty-fire from Yin deficiency. Grey-moist → retention of phlegm-fluids, cold-damp blockage
Interior (heat, cold). Black-dry coating + fissure (bumps if severe) → extreme heat drying up body fluids. Black-slippery → replete cold + declined Yang

Whether the surface of the tongue underneath the coating can be seen. Reflects level (shallow/deep) of evil Qi and state of ST Qi. Thin (薄) → normal, intact right Qi, mild evil Qi (exterior, mild interior). Thick (厚) → interior, replete evil Qi, phlegm-fluids, damp, food stagnation. Thin becoming thick → evil Qi getting stronger. Thick becoming thin → recovering right Qi

Normal is neither dry nor excessively moist (slippery). Moist (潤) → normal, undamaged body fluids even in disease, heat evil entering blood level and streaming Yin Qi (uncommon). Slippery (濕滑) → Yang deficiency, cold, phlegm-damp, retention of water-damp. Dry (燥) → heat damaging body fluids, Yin fluids exhaustion, Yang deficiency (unable to transform fluids), dry (autumn) Qi damaging LU, damp evil entering Qi level (uncommon)

Thick coating with heavy quality. Rotten (腐): chunks of bean-curd (tofu)-like coating, easily removed if scraped → heat, retention of food, turbid phlegm, (internal abscess, damp-heat). Greasy (膩): oily and sticky coating, unable to scrape off → retention of turbid-damp, phlegm-fluids, retention of food, damp-heat. White-greasy + non-dry → SP deficiency + damp, turbid damp, cold-damp. White-greasy-sticky + dry → SP/ST damp-heat, phlegm-heat, food stagnation

Coating on entire tongue (全): thorough spread of evil Qi, phlegm-damp blockage in the middle Jiao. Coating on part of the tongue (偏苔): close to tip (LU/HT) → exterior or relatively shallow level; close to root (KI/BL) → interior; scanty coating in middle to root (SP/ST, KI) → ST Yang unable to steam upward + KI Yin unable to nourish the upper → damage to Yin, essence, blood, and Qi; thick coating in the middle → phlegm-damp, accumulation in ST & intestine; coating on the sides (LV/GB) → Shaoyang syndrome, LV/GB damp-heat

Glossy/mirror (光剝舌, 光滑舌, 鏡面舌): no coating on the entire tongue → Yin deficiency. Partial loss of coating: falling flower (花剝苔) = glossy missing spots + scanty coating elsewhere; geographical (地圖舌) = glossy missing spots + thick coating elsewhere → ST Qi and Yin deficiency. Greasy coating in the remaining area → turbid-phlegm + damaged right Qi
Loud sound → excess. Weak sound → deficiency

items.t: Sudden change (rough, coarse) or loss of voice → exterior (wind-heat, wind-cold, turbid-phlegm)
Gradual change or loss of voice → empty-heat from LU/KI Yin deficiency damaging LU (Yin, body fluids)
Other causes of voice loss → sudden anger, pregnancy (blocking KI essence from reaching the throat)
Rough, forceful, rapid breathing → excess, heat
Weak, feeble, slow breathing → deficiency, cold
Panting (Chuan, 喘: difficult, short, & rapid breathing)
items.detail: Excess → LU (heat, phlegm). Deficiency → deficiency (LU, KI) (KI unable to "receive Qi")

items.t: Wheezing (Xiao, 哮: rapid breathing with phlegm sound, 痰鳴)
items.detail: Phlegm-fluids (underlying condition) + external invasion of evil Qi (triggering factor). [Note] Panting and wheezing (asthma) usually happen together but need to be differentiated

LU Qi unable to properly disperse and descend → pathological upward movement of Qi → rapid-short breathing. Excess → phlegm-fluids in the chest, exterior wind knotting the skin & pores. Deficiency → empty-heat from Yin deficiency

items.t: Short Qi (短氣: hasty-short breathing with discontinued breathing)
items.detail: Excess → fluids retention in the chest. Deficiency → LU Qi deficiency

items.t: Less Qi (少氣, 氣微: weak breathing) → deficiency
Coughing (咳嗽), Vomiting (嘔吐), Hiccup (呃逆), Belching (噯氣)
items.detail: Loud → excess. Weak → deficiency

items.t: Emitting a long, deep, audible breath to relieve the uncomfortable stuffy sensation in the chest → LV Qi stagnation, HT
Rumbling or gurgling noise made by the movement of fluid and gas in the intestines → excess (phlegm-fluids, cold, damp, wind), deficiency (weak middle Jiao)

items.detail: Strong-foul smell → heat. Absence of smell → cold
Bad breath in general → indigestion (sour smell), ST heat (dirty smell), intestinal abscess or ulcer (rotten smell), [cavities]
Strong foul smell → heat. Fish smell → cold
Strong-foul smell in stools → heat. Foul smell in leukorrhea → heat. Yellow urine with strong smell → damp-heat. Fish smell in stools → cold. Fish smell in leukorrhea → cold

items.t: Asking is a very important part of TCM diagnosis
Subjective feeling and objective findings of the patient — all from the patient's point of view

items.detail: Cold & heat; sweat; head & body; stool & urine; food; chest; hearing; thirst; chronic condition(s); cause(s). Also: changes from taking herbs; menses for women; measles and chickenpox for children

items.cn: 一問寒熱二問汗, 三問頭身四問便, 五問飲食六問胸, 七聾八渴俱當辨, 九問舊病十問因, 再兼服藥參機變, 婦女尤必問經期, 遲速閉崩皆可見, 再添片語告兒科, 天花麻疹全占驗

items.t: Name, age, gender, marriage, occupation, race, ...
Dietary habits, exercise, rest, emotional state (happy, sad, stressed, ...), ...

blocks.heading: Questions related to the present condition(s)

items.t: When the condition(s) began and how it has changed → clues to pattern differentiation

items.detail: Cold-damp weather → cold. Emotional stress → LV Qi stagnation. Acute tinnitus → excess (LV fire uprising). Chronic tinnitus → deficiency (KI Yin deficiency)

items.t: Sensation of heat or cold is one of the most important concepts in TCM

items.detail: Chills / aversion to cold (Wu Han, 惡寒): feeling cold + like to cover up & prefer warmth, but condition NOT alleviated by covering up or warmth. Afraid of cold (Wei Han, 畏寒) and aversion to wind (Wu Feng, 惡風): feeling cold + condition alleviated by avoiding cold, covering up, and/or warmth
Fever / heat effusion (Fa Re, 發熱): feeling hot (entire body or local area) + actual rise in temperature or normal temperature

items.t: 4 possible combinations of heat and cold in TCM
items.detail: Simultaneous fever and chills (惡寒發熱); chills without fever (但寒不熱); fever without chills (但熱不寒); alternating fever and chills (寒熱往來)

blocks.heading: Simultaneous fever and chills (惡寒發熱)

items.t: Having a sensation of hot (fever) and cold (chills) at the same time
1) Patterns and pathogenesis — exterior pattern (外感表證)
items.detail: Struggle between the external invasion of evil Qi (邪氣) and right Qi (正氣; defensive Qi, Wei Qi, 衛氣). Evil Qi obstructing the right Qi → right Qi unable to properly function/warm the body → chills. Struggle between right Qi and evil Qi → closed sweat pores (玄府) and depressed (stagnated) right Qi → fever

items.t: [Note] Strength of evil Qi and/or right Qi
items.detail: Severe fever and chills → fierce struggle between strong evil Qi and right Qi. Light fever and chills → relatively mild evil Qi and/or weak right Qi

Predominant fever + slight chills → exterior heat pattern (wind-heat) (表熱, 風熱). Predominant cold + slight fever → exterior cold pattern (wind-cold) (表寒, 風寒): exterior cold-excess (Taiyang Shang Han, 太陽傷寒) → tight pulse; exterior cold-deficiency (Taiyang Zhong Feng, 太陽中風) → moderate pulse, sweating

items.t: No heat but cold → feeling cold without any heat sensation + cold alleviated by covering up or warmth = afraid of cold (Wei Han, 畏寒)
1) Patterns and pathogenesis — interior cold (裏寒)
items.detail: Yang deficiency, unable to warm the exterior of the body: chronic + chills, weakness, deep-slow-forceless pulse → empty-cold (虛寒). Direct cold invasion into the interior, damaging Yang: acute + cold-severe pain, deep-slow-forceful pulse → excess cold (實寒)
Special case of cold limbs. Qi stagnation (氣厥), heat stagnation (熱厥), or phlegm stagnation (痰厥) causing the reversal cold

blocks.heading: Fever without chills (但熱不寒) — interior heat (裏熱)

items.t: Feeling hot (objective and/or subjective) without any cold sensation

items.detail: External invasion of evil Qi entering the interior (exterior evil transforming into heat as it enters, or wind-heat entering the interior) → fierce struggle between evil Qi and right Qi → fever. High fever, red face, thirst & preferring cold water, profuse sweating, large-surging pulse. [Note] When external cold or heat directly attacks the interior it is called "direct strike" (直中) — usually with cold
Heat sensation occurring only at a certain time of day, like the ebb and flow of the tide

items.t: Yin deficiency tidal fever (陰虛潮熱)
items.detail: Empty-heat from Yin deficiency. Low degree fever during afternoon or night + red cheeks (顴紅), night sweating (盜汗). Relatively weakened right Qi (Yang) in the afternoon → relatively strengthened evil Qi. Yang entering into Yin level at night → steaming Yin → sensation of heat emitting from bone to skin, night sweating

Yangming Fu excess pattern (陽明腑實證). High fever during 3–5pm (日晡) + abdominal distention, constipation. Yangming channel's own Qi becomes stronger during 3–5pm → stronger struggle between right Qi and stagnated excess evil Qi in Yangming

Damp-warm disease. High fever during afternoon (generally low degree at first touch, getting hotter soon after) + heavy sensation of head and body. Damp being sticky evil, obstructing and hiding heat → generally low degree fever; relatively weakened right Qi (Yang) in the afternoon becomes less potent against damp-warm evil → higher fever
Fever from Yin deficiency empty-heat (陰虛發熱) — same as Yin deficiency tidal fever

Prolonged low degree fever + shortness of breath, spontaneous sweating, fatigue. SP Qi deficiency unable to properly spread Yang Qi to warm the body → Yang Qi congested (lack of strength) at the skin exterior and struggling

items.t: Summer heat of small children (小兒夏季熱)
items.detail: Prolonged fever under severe summer weather, restlessness, agitation, thirst, no sweating, frequent urination, fever spontaneously disappearing as cooling fall weather arrives. Unable to respond to severe summer weather because of insufficient Qi and Yin of small children
True cold and false heat (眞寒假熱) → separating Yang (格陽), deserting Yang (戴陽)

blocks.heading: Alternating fever and chills (寒熱往來)

items.t: Alternating hot and cold sensation = half exterior and half interior (半表半裏)

items.detail: Alternating fever and chills without fixed time interval + bitter taste in the mouth, dry throat, dizziness, hypochondriac pain, poor appetite, wiry pulse. External evil Qi trying to enter the interior and right Qi trying to stop it → struggle at the half exterior/half interior → fever when right Qi is fighting to drive out evil Qi + chills when evil Qi prevails
Alternating fever and chills with a fixed time interval (1/day, 1–2/day, 1/3-day, etc.) + headache, thirst, profuse sweating. Same patho-mechanism as Shaoyang pattern

items.t: Sweats are body fluids steamed by Yang Qi and excreted through the sweat pores
Under normal condition, sweating regulates and harmonizes Ying and Wei (營衛) and nourishes/moistens the skin
Under pathological condition, abnormal sweating or no sweating may occur
items.detail: Observe absence/presence of sweats, time and location of sweating, amount of sweats; differentiate exterior-interior, excess-deficiency, and/or heat-cold

items.t: 1) Wind-cold (風寒) — exterior excess (Taiyang cold damage, 太陽傷寒)
items.detail: Fever and chills (aversion to cold), no sweating, severe headache and stiff neck, floating-tight pulse. Severe struggle between evil cold and right Qi obstructing the skin + cold (Yin) evil constricting the pores → no sweats

items.t: Exterior deficiency (Taiyang wind-strike, 太陽中風)
items.detail: Fever and chills (aversion to wind), sweating, floating-moderate pulse. Weak Wei Qi unable to contain sweat while fighting against evil cold
Fever and chills (fever more), sweating, headache, sore throat, floating-rapid pulse. Heat (Yang) evil at the skin opens up the pores → sweat flowing outwards

blocks.heading: Interior conditions and sweating
blocks.lead: Cold or heat evil + excess or deficiency of Yin or Yang → sweating

items.detail: Easily sweating without excessive activity + afraid of cold, fatigue, weak. Yang deficiency (陽虛): weak Wei Yang (衛陽) unable to secure the skin exterior and pores → leaking out sweats

items.t: 2) Night / stealing sweating (Dao Han, 盜汗)
items.detail: Sweating when sleeping and no more sweating upon waking + tidal fever, red cheeks. Yin deficiency (陰虛): Yang moving into the interior while sleeping (relatively unsecure exterior) + empty-heat from Yin deficiency (empty-heat steaming out sweats) → upon waking, Yang moves to secure the exterior → no more sweating

items.t: 3) Profuse / excessive sweating (Da Han, 大汗)
items.detail: From 2 different conditions: excess heat (實熱), Yang collapse (亡陽)
Profuse and non-stopping sweats + severe fever, red face, thirst with preference for cold water, large-surging pulse. Excessive interior heat steaming out body fluids
Profuse cold-oily sweats + pale-white facial complexion, reversal cold, weak-thin pulse. Yang Qi escaping outward and unable to check the exterior → leaking out sweats

Sweating after chills and shivering — result of fierce struggle between right Qi and evil Qi → indication of recovery or worsening. Receding fever, moderate pulse after shivering sweating → right Qi prevailing → recovery. High fever, pulse of disease → evil Qi prevailing → worsening condition
Relatively more sweating from the head and/or neck. 3 types: evil heat in the upper Jiao (上焦邪熱), damp-heat in the middle Jiao (中焦濕熱上蒸), and empty Yang floating upward (虛陽上越)

items.t: Evil heat in the upper Jiao (上焦邪熱)
items.detail: Profuse sweating from head and face + red face, agitation, thirst, red tip with thin-yellow tongue coating, rapid pulse. Evil heat in the upper Jiao streaming along the Yang channels

items.t: Damp-heat in the middle Jiao (中焦濕熱上蒸)
items.detail: More sweating from head and face + heavy sensation in head and body, low degree fever, stuffy epigastric area, yellow-greasy tongue coating. Damp-heat steaming upwards along the Yang channels

items.t: Empty Yang floating upward (虛陽上越)
items.detail: Oily sweats from the forehead + reversal cold of the limbs, panting, feeble pulse. Separating Yin and Yang → leaking body fluids following Yang → critical

Sweating from one side (left, right, upper, or lower) only, no sweats from the other (affected) side. Wind-stroke, Wei syndrome (atrophy, 痿證). Qi and blood unable to flow freely due to blockage of channels on the affected side → sweating from the healthy side only
Relatively more sweating from the 5-center (palms, soles, chest) → Yin deficiency

items.t: 4) Sweating from the 4 limbs, hands, or feet (手足汗)
items.detail: Relatively more sweating from the limbs or hands & feet. SP controls the 4 limbs → disorders of SP/ST cause sweating from the 4 limbs

items.t: Headache, dizziness, joint pain, body ache, heaviness sensation, lower back pain, etc.

rows: Anterior hair line to the supraorbital ridge
Temporal area, extra point Taiyang (太陽)
Back of the head to the neck

blocks.heading: Headache — nature, time, and other patterns

items.t: Acute onset + relatively severe pain + non-stopping headache → exterior excess
items.detail: Aggravated by cold/wind + pain at the nape area → wind-cold. Aggravated by heat + red face and eyes → wind-heat. Band-wrapped-around-the-head sensation + heaviness → wind-damp

items.t: Chronic + dull pain + on-and-off headache → interior deficiency
items.detail: Continuous pain + aggravated by exertion/fatigue (+tinnitus) → Qi deficiency. Headache with dizziness + pale-white complexion → blood deficiency. Headache with empty-headed sensation + weak/sore lower back and/or knees → KI deficiency

Stabbing, splitting, localized (fixed location), intermittent headache → blood stasis. Headache with heavy and unclear sensation of the head → phlegm, damp. Distending and throbbing headache, dizziness → LV Yang rising. Headache behind the eyes (deep inside) → LV (Yang rising, blood deficiency)
Acute-sharp → external invasion of wind. Chronic-dull → KI deficiency

items.t: Mild dizziness → alleviated when closing the eyes
Severe dizziness (vertigo) → feeling of spinning/whirling, unsteadiness, nausea/vomiting, sweating, fainting if severe
1) Excess — factors blocking the clear orifices
items.detail: Dizziness with distending headache + tinnitus, bitter taste in mouth, dry throat → LV Yang rising (肝陽上亢). Dizziness with heavy sensation + stuffy chest, nausea/vomiting → phlegm-damp (痰濕內阻)

items.t: 2) Deficiency — unable to nourish the brain (sea of marrow)
items.detail: Dizziness with eye floaters (眼花), dizzy when standing up or tired + palpitation, insomnia, pale complexion, dry-dull skin → Qi and blood deficiency. Dizziness with tinnitus + forgetfulness, dry eyes, sore/weak lower back and knees → LV/KI deficiency

Muscular and/or joint pain all around the body, usually from external invasion of wind. Acute: wind-cold or wind-damp invasion blocking Qi and blood flow in the channels. Chronic: Qi and blood deficiency → pain (discomfort) + fatigue

Sensation of heaviness in the head and body (not due to physical weight). Excess: wind-damp — damp (sticky-greasy-heavy) lodged on the channels, Yang Qi unable to circulate; heaviness + stuffy sensation in chest/epigastric area, greasy coating, loose stools. Deficiency: SP Qi deficiency, unable to properly transform/transport food essence, unable to nourish flesh and 4 limbs; heaviness + like to lie down, tired, dislike to talk, weak and short breathing

items.t: 3) Painful limbs (四肢痛) — painful joints (關節疼痛)
items.detail: Usually Bi syndrome (painful obstruction syndrome, 痹證), wind-cold-damp. Moving/wind Bi (行痹, 風痹) → moving pain. Painful/cold Bi (痛痹, 寒痹) → severe pain. Fixed/damp Bi (着痹, 濕痹) → heavy and lodged pain. Heat Bi (熱痹) → painful and red-swollen joints; either by transformation from wind-cold-damp or wind-damp-heat

Lingering dull pain + weak and sore lower back and knees → KI deficiency (insufficient bone marrow, unable to nourish the lower back). Severe pain with cold-heavy sensation, aggravated by cold or damp weather, alleviated by warmth → damp-cold (attacking the lower back, blocking the channels, inhibiting flow of Qi and blood). Stabbing pain, fixed location, dislike pressure, difficulty turning the waist → blood stasis (traumatic injuries)
Excess → LV wind, phlegm (usually one side of the body). Deficiency → blood deficiency (usually bilateral)

blocks.heading: Chest and abdomen (胸腹) — basic divisions

items.detail: Chest (Xiong, 胸): above the diaphragm (膈). Abdomen (Fu, 腹): below the diaphragm
Chest (narrow sense): area above RE17 (Danzhong, 膻中), between the 2 nipples. Ying Xiong (膺胸): area between RE17 (膻中) and RE15 (Jiuwei, 鳩尾)

Upper abdomen (上腹): above the umbilicus, RE08 (神闕) to RE15 (鳩尾) — epigastric area (Weiwan, 胃脘) between RE15 and RE10 (下脘); below HT (Xinxia, 心下) between RE15 and RE12 (中脘); large abdomen (Dufu, 大腹) between RE10 and RE08. Lower abdomen (下腹): below the umbilicus, RE08 to RE02 (曲骨) — small abdomen (Xiaofu, 小腹) middle part; lesser abdomen (Shaofu, 少腹) sides
Area surrounded by the costal margin (rib-ends) and rib cage, below the nipple. Ji Xie (季脇): area immediately below the costal margin
LU, HT, PC, Dan Zhong (膻中), Xuli (虛裏)

items.t: Chest pain radiating to the shoulder and upper arm — Chest Bi (Xiong Bi, 胸痹)
items.detail: Lack of chest Yang leading to phlegm-damp accumulation, or Qi deficiency with blood stasis → sluggish flow of Qi and blood

items.t: Sharp pain radiating to the scapular area + bluish complexion → angina pectoris (眞心痛)
items.detail: Acute obstruction of HT vessels

items.t: Chest pain, high fever, red face, panting, flaring of alae nasi → LU excess-heat (風熱犯肺)
Chest pain, tidal fever, night sweating, coughing blood-tainted phlegm → LU Yin deficiency (虛火傷肺)
Chest pain, fever, coughing/vomiting pus and blood-tainted, fish-smell phlegm → LU abscess (肺癰)
Fixed + stabbing nature of chest pain → blood stasis (瘀血)
Pain and fullness in the chest, sighing, angry easily → Qi stagnation (氣滯)
Stuffy sensation, panting, wheezing, whitish phlegm → phlegm-damp invading LU (痰濕犯肺)
Fullness sensation in the chest without pain → Pi syndrome (Distention syndrome, 痞證)

items.detail: LV, GB → conditions of LV, GB, blood, and/or Qi

items.t: Distending hypochondriac pain, sighing, angry easily → LV Qi stagnation (肝氣鬱結)
Burning hypochondriac pain, red face and eyes → stagnated LV fire (肝火鬱滯)
Distending hypochondriac pain, yellow face and eyes → LV/GB damp-heat (肝膽濕熱)
Hypochondriac pain + fixed location, stabbing nature → blood stasis (瘀血)
Hypochondriac pain, full sensation in the rib cage, aggravated by cough/turning/breathing → Xuan Yin (fluids retention in the chest and hypochondriac region, 懸飮)
Distending hypochondriac pain, alternating fever and chills → Shaoyang syndrome (少陽病)

items.detail: Epigastric area (Weiwan, 胃脘) → Yangming ST (ST receives and ferments food/drink 胃主受納腐熟水穀; ST Qi descends 胃氣降). Large abdomen (Dufu, 大腹) → SP. Intestines → middle portion, centered around the umbilicus. Small abdomen (Xiaofu, 小腹) → BL, uterus. Lesser abdomen (Shaofu, 少腹) → LV/GB channels, external genital. CHONG Mai (衝脈) → vertically & bilaterally beside the umbilicus. REN Mai (任脈) → vertically through the umbilicus

items.t: Severe epigastric cold-pain, alleviated by warmth → cold invading ST (寒邪犯胃)
Epigastric burning pain, easily hungry, bad breath, constipation → ST fire (胃火熾盛)
Distending epigastric pain aggravated by anger, belching → ST Qi stagnation (胃腑氣滯)
Stabbing epigastric pain, fixed location → ST blood stasis (胃腑瘀血)
Dull epigastric pain, likes warmth/pressure, vomits clear fluid → ST Yang deficiency (胃陽虛)
Burning epigastric pain, hungry easily but poor appetite, red tongue with scanty coating → ST Yin deficiency (胃陰虛)
Dull large-abdominal pain, likes warmth & pressure, loose stools → SP/ST empty-cold (脾胃虛寒)
Small abdominal distending pain, inhibited urination → Stranguria (difficult urination, 癃閉)
Stabbing pain at the small abdominal area, no urination issues → accumulation of blood (blood stasis in the small abdomen, 蓄血)
Cold pain in the lesser abdomen, pulling sensation of the genital area → cold in LV channel (寒凝肝脈)
Pain around the umbilicus, raised lump moving on touch → accumulation of parasites (蟲積)

items.detail: Severe-distending acute pain, aggravated by pressure, painful upon eating, alleviated after bowel movement → excess syndrome (實證). Gradually developed dull pain, alleviated by pressure, less painful upon eating → deficiency syndrome (虛證). Cramping pain, alleviated by warmth → cold syndrome (寒證). Burning sensation with pain, alleviated by cold → heat syndrome (熱證)

items.t: KI opens into the ears (腎開竅於耳); hand & foot Shaoyang channels circle around the ears
LV opens into the eyes (目爲肝之竅); Zangfu organs' essence flows into the eyes

items.detail: Acute, loud, high pitch, aggravated by pressing the ears → excess: LV/GB/SJ fire flaring up along the channels, or excessive SP damp blocking the clear Yang going up to nourish the orifices. Gradual, dull, low pitch, alleviated by pressing the ears → deficiency (KI essence)

items.t: 2) Deafness — partial or complete hearing loss (耳聾)
items.detail: Acute → excess: wind-cold attack blocking Shaoyang channel's Qi, or warm disease with fire evil misting the clear orifices (relatively easier to treat than deficiency type). Gradual → deficiency: chronic or severe condition (deficiency of HT Qi, KI essence), aging (Qi and essence deficiency) — difficult to treat
External wind invasion, heat in LV channel → excess. Deficiency of lower origin (essence deficiency) → deficiency
Red-painful eyes → wind-heat, LV fire. [Note] Glaucoma (綠/靑/黃風內障): painful eyes with headache, blurred vision, nausea, vomiting → LV/GB fire, empty-fire, wind-heat
Eye floaters, dry eyes, blurred vision → deficiency (blood, LV blood, KI essence, Qi), aging
LV deficiency

blocks.heading: Food and taste (飮食, 口味)

items.t: "ST is the sea of food and drink."
items.detail: <Ling Shu: Chapter 33 (靈樞: 海論)>

items.t: ST controls receiving, rotting, and fermenting of food and drink; SP controls transforming and transporting of food and drink
ST and SP are the root of the post-heaven

items.detail: Excess → aggravated by eating. Deficiency → alleviated by eating. Poor appetite → weak SP/ST. Always hungry → ST heat. Feeling full or distended after eating → food stagnation

No thought of food, dislike even smelling food if severe. Emaciation, weakness, distended abdomen, loose stools, pale tongue → SP/ST Qi deficiency. Stuffy epigastric sensation, fullness even with small intake, heaviness (head, body), loose stools, greasy coating → damp in SP (summer-damp invasion). Dislike greasy food, jaundice, hypochondriac pain, low degree fever → LV/GB damp-heat. Dislike to eat, belching with sour taste, abdominal distention with pain, thick-rotten coating → food stagnation. Pregnancy → dislike to eat, nausea, vomiting, slippery-rapid pulse

items.t: 3) Strong appetite with quick hunger (多食易飢)
items.detail: Like to eat, eat a lot, hungry again soon after eating. Strong appetite, easily hungry + thirst, agitation, red tongue with yellow coating, bad breath, constipation → ST fire. Strong appetite, easily hungry + loose stools → SP/ST disharmony (strong ST constraining SP, 脾約證)

items.t: 4) Hungry but no appetite (飢不欲食)
items.detail: Feel hungry but no desire to eat. Chronic uncomfortable (burning) sensation in ST, red tongue with scanty coating, thin-rapid pulse → insufficiency of ST Yin
Parasite accumulation in small children. Pregnancy → like to eat sour food, no menses, nausea. Deserted middle (除中) → sudden increase of appetite in a severe condition, emergency

items.t: Having a special or strange taste in the mouth
No sense of taste (bland, 口淡乏味) + poor appetite → SP/ST Qi deficiency, (HT)
Sweet or greasy taste in the mouth → SP/ST damp-heat
Abundant sour taste → accumulation of heat in LV/ST
Sour and rotten taste → food stagnation
Bitter taste → Heat (LV/GB heat, HT fire)
Sweet for SP, sour for LV, etc. → deficiency of the corresponding Zangfu organ

blocks.heading: Thirst (渴症)
blocks.lead: Insufficient body fluids or blockage of fluids distribution → thirst

items.detail: No damage to the body fluids → normal, cold, no prominent heat evil yet

items.t: Thirst with drinking a lot (口渴多飮) — severe damage of body fluids
